MT-software-extensions.name: Installed-extension authority audit
MT-software-extensions.authorization: Authorized assessment of owned/contracted endpoints only, signed RoE. Read-only.
MT-software-extensions.setup: Point at a local sample of installed browser/IDE extension manifests (manifest.json files) on an owned endpoint.
MT-software-extensions.success_criterion: An installed extension is found holding all-sites/cookies/webRequest authority that persists across restarts beyond the install-time consent.
MT-software-extensions.safety: Read-only manifest audit; no extension is installed, modified, or exercised.

MT-network-boundary-device.name: Boundary-device posture-vs-declared audit
MT-network-boundary-device.authorization: Authorized assessment of owned/contracted network devices only, signed RoE. Read-only config review.
MT-network-boundary-device.setup: Provide a sample device running-config export (no live device change).
MT-network-boundary-device.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Read-only audit: detect a boundary device whose ACTUAL config contradicts its declared hardened posture
# (weakened crypto, route/NAT bridging across segments, or an unverified system image).
SAMPLE_CONFIG = """
crypto isakmp policy 10
 encryption des
ip route 10.0.0.0 255.0.0.0 192.0.2.9
boot system flash unverified-image.bin
"""
flags = []
if "encryption des" in SAMPLE_CONFIG or " md5" in SAMPLE_CONFIG: flags.append("weakened-encryption (T1600)")
if "ip route" in SAMPLE_CONFIG and "192.0.2." in SAMPLE_CONFIG: flags.append("cross-segment route/NAT bridge (T1599)")
if "unverified-image" in SAMPLE_CONFIG or "no verify" in SAMPLE_CONFIG: flags.append("unverified system image (T1601)")
print("VULNERABLE: boundary device contradicts declared posture -> %s" % flags if flags else "PASS: posture consistent")
MT-network-boundary-device.success_criterion: The device's running config reveals weakened crypto, cross-segment bridging, or an unverified image while it advertises a hardened posture.
MT-network-boundary-device.safety: Read-only config review against a sample export; no device is reconfigured.
